hypothesis: If an LLM-based generative agent is augmented with external tools (short- and long-term memory, periodic table, knowledge base) and coupled to DiffCSP for crystal structure generation and iComFormer for property evaluation, then it will achieve higher success rates at producing novel, compositionally valid inorganic materials with target formation energies (within ±0.25 eV/atom) and superior V.U.N. scores compared to tool-free LLM variants and MatterGen, because tool-guided planning and iterative feedback enable more directed, higher-quality proposals.
